title: Deployment
content:
### Vercel Deployment

Vercel is the platform built by the creators of Next.js, offering the best deployment experience:

1. Push your code to GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket
2. Import the repository on [vercel.com](https://vercel.com)
3. Vercel detects Next.js automatically and configures the build
4. Deploy with `git push`

**Benefits**: Automatic ISR, Edge Functions, Analytics, Preview Deployments

### Other Platforms

#### Netlify

```bash
npm install @netlify/plugin-nextjs
```

Create `netlify.toml`:
```toml
[build]
  command = "npm run build"
  publish = ".next"

[[plugins]]
  package = "@netlify/plugin-nextjs"
```

#### AWS (Serverless)

Deploy using Serverless Framework or SST:
```bash
npm install serverless-nextjs
npx serverless
```

#### Docker

```dockerfile
FROM node:18-alpine AS base

FROM base AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci

FROM base AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM base AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production

COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder /app/.next/static ./.next/static

EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
```

Enable standalone output:
```js
// next.config.js
module.exports = {
  output: 'standalone',
};
```

### Build Configuration

```js
// next.config.js
module.exports = {
  compress: true,
  poweredByHeader: false,
  generateEtags: true,
};
```

### Environment Configuration

Set environment variables in your deployment platform:

- Vercel: Project Settings > Environment Variables
- Netlify: Site Settings > Build & Deploy > Environment
- AWS: Parameter Store or Secrets Manager

### Post-Deployment Checks

- Verify custom domain SSL certificate
- Check CDN caching behavior
- Monitor error rates
- Set up analytics
- Configure preview deployments for PRs
codeExample:
# Dockerfile for Next.js
FROM node:18-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app

ENV NODE_ENV=production

COPY public ./public
COPY .next/standalone ./
COPY .next/static ./.next/static

EXPOSE 3000

ENV PORT=3000
ENV HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"

CMD ["node", "server.js"]

quiz.question: Which deployment platform is created by the same team behind Next.js?
What config enables Docker-optimized standalone output?
